description: Tracks which papers have been fact-checked against their original PDFs in the Psi Research Library. Each entry records the paper ID, the date it was last verified, and any issues found and fixed.

checks.title: Does Psi Exist? Replicable Evidence for an Anomalous Process of Information Transfer
fixes: plain_summary: '500 billion' corrected to '48 billion' (p=2.1e-11)

checks.title: Feeling the Future: Experimental Evidence for Anomalous Retroactive Influences on Cognition and Affect
Predictive Physiological Anticipation Preceding Seemingly Unpredictable Stimuli
Consciousness and the Double-Slit Interference Pattern: Six Experiments
fixes: abstract: EEG correlation now notes post-hoc status and full-sample p=0.09
plain_summary: 'one in a million' corrected to 'one in 167,000' (p=6e-6)
plain_summary: absorption corrected from confirmed to marginal (p=0.07)
plain_summary: added meditation-experience null (p=0.75)
abstract + plain_summary: added Experiment 4 retrocausal design

fixes: abstract + notes + plain_summary: removed '$2.4 million' (not in PDF)
notes + tags: removed 'nocebo' attribution (word not in paper)
replication_map: removed 'nocebo' from description

checks.title: Feeling the Future: A Meta-Analysis of 90 Experiments
fixes: abstract: z=6.33 corrected to z=6.40 (per PDF abstract)
controversy 02: 'd = 0.09' corrected to 'Hedges' g = 0.09'

checks.title: Correcting the Past: Failures to Replicate Psi

replication_map: 'multiple labs' corrected to 'single team across multiple sites'

fixes: abstract + effect_size + plain_summary: P=3e-4 corrected to P=5.6e-4 (conservative permutation value from PDF)

fixes: replication_map: "sensory leakage" and "statistical methods" replaced with actual concerns (randomization testing, target frequency artifact, experimenter prompting) — Hyman explicitly disclaims sensory leakage in footnote 2
